rules.name: allow-filesystem-reads
rules.description: Allow reading files, listing directories, and querying metadata anywhere on the filesystem.
rules.principle: Broad read access: Reading files, listing directories, and querying metadata is permitted anywhere.
rules.reason: Constitution explicitly permits reading files and listing directories anywhere on the filesystem.

rules.name: allow-list-allowed-directories
rules.description: Allow querying which directories the filesystem server can access.
rules.principle: Broad read access: Reading files, listing directories, and querying metadata is permitted anywhere.
rules.reason: Listing allowed directories is a read-only metadata query explicitly permitted.

rules.name: escalate-filesystem-writes
rules.description: Escalate any file write, edit, or create operation for human approval.
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: Constitution requires human approval before any write or create operation.

rules.name: escalate-filesystem-move
rules.description: Escalate any file move/rename operation for human approval.
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: Move operations modify the filesystem by deleting the source and creating the destination, requiring human approval.

rules.name: allow-git-read-only-tools
rules.description: Allow reading git log, status, diff, blame, show, reflog, and changelog analysis.
rules.principle: The agent is allowed to read git log, status, diff, and branch information.
rules.reason: Constitution explicitly permits reading git log, status, diff, and branch information.

rules.name: allow-git-set-working-dir
rules.description: Allow setting or clearing the git working directory for navigation.
rules.principle: The agent is allowed to set the git working directory for navigation.
rules.reason: Constitution explicitly permits setting the git working directory for navigation.

rules.name: allow-git-wrapup-instructions
rules.description: Allow querying git wrap-up workflow instructions (read-only, no side effects).
rules.principle: Read-only exploration: The agent may only observe and query.
rules.reason: git_wrapup_instructions is a read-only query with no side effects.

rules.name: allow-git-branch-read
rules.description: Allow listing git branches (read-only).
rules.principle: The agent is allowed to read git log, status, diff, and branch information.
rules.reason: Constitution permits reading branch information; listing only activates read-path role.

rules.name: allow-git-remote-list
rules.description: Allow listing git remotes (read-only, no remote URL argument present).
rules.principle: The agent is allowed to read git log, status, diff, and branch information.
rules.reason: Listing remotes only activates read-path; constitution only requires approval for mutations to remote config.

rules.name: escalate-git-remote-mutations
rules.description: Escalate adding, removing, or renaming git remotes (when a remote URL is provided).
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before adding, removing, or renaming git remotes.
rules.reason: Constitution requires human approval before modifying git remote configuration.

rules.name: allow-git-fetch-from-github
rules.description: Allow git fetch from GitHub remotes.
rules.principle: GitHub read access: Querying GitHub repositories, issues, and pull requests is permitted for reading.
rules.reason: Constitution permits GitHub read access; git fetch downloads objects without merging.

rules.name: allow-git-clone-from-github
rules.description: Allow git clone from GitHub.
rules.principle: GitHub read access: Querying GitHub repositories, issues, and pull requests is permitted for reading.
rules.reason: Constitution permits GitHub read access; cloning from GitHub is a read operation.

rules.name: allow-git-stash-list
rules.description: Allow listing git stashes (read-only stash inspection with no write-history argument active).
rules.principle: The agent is allowed to read git log, status, diff, and branch information.
rules.reason: Stash listing is read-only; constitution permits read-only git inspection.

rules.name: escalate-git-stash-mutations
rules.description: Escalate git stash push/pop/apply/drop operations that modify git state.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state.
rules.reason: Stash push/pop operations modify git state and require human approval.

rules.name: allow-git-clean-dry-run
rules.description: Allow git clean in dry-run mode (preview only, read-path role only, no delete-path active).
rules.principle: Read-only exploration: The agent may only observe and query.
rules.reason: git_clean dry-run only reads the working tree without deleting anything.

rules.name: escalate-git-clean-actual
rules.description: Escalate git clean when it would actually delete files (delete-path role active).
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: git_clean deletes untracked files from the working directory and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-checkout
rules.description: Escalate git checkout operations that modify git state.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state (commit, checkout, merge, rebase).
rules.reason: git_checkout modifies git working tree state and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-commit
rules.description: Escalate git commit operations that modify git history.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state (commit, checkout, merge, rebase).
rules.reason: git_commit modifies git history and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-merge
rules.description: Escalate git merge operations that modify git history.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state (commit, checkout, merge, rebase).
rules.reason: git_merge modifies git history and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-rebase
rules.description: Escalate git rebase operations that modify git history.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state (commit, checkout, merge, rebase).
rules.reason: git_rebase modifies git history and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-reset
rules.description: Escalate git reset operations that modify git state.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state (commit, checkout, merge, rebase).
rules.reason: git_reset modifies git state and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-cherry-pick
rules.description: Escalate git cherry-pick operations that modify git history.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state (commit, checkout, merge, rebase).
rules.reason: git_cherry_pick modifies git history and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-pull
rules.description: Escalate git pull operations that modify git state.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state (commit, checkout, merge, rebase).
rules.reason: git_pull fetches and merges, modifying git state, and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-push
rules.description: Escalate git push operations to GitHub for human approval.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before any write, create, delete, or push operation.
rules.reason: Constitution requires human approval before push operations even to GitHub.

rules.name: escalate-git-add
rules.description: Escalate git add (staging files modifies git index state).
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state.
rules.reason: Staging files modifies the git index and requires human approval per the constitution.

rules.name: escalate-git-tag
rules.description: Escalate git tag operations that create or delete tags.
rules.principle: The agent must ask for approval before modifying git state.
rules.reason: Creating or deleting tags modifies git state and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-worktree
rules.description: Escalate git worktree operations that create or delete working trees.
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: git_worktree can create or delete working trees and requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-init
rules.description: Escalate git init that creates a new repository.
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: git_init creates a new git repository on disk and requires human approval.

rules.name: allow-github-read-operations
rules.description: Allow read-only GitHub API operations: listing/reading issues, PRs, repos, commits, tags, releases, users, teams, and code search.
rules.principle: GitHub read access: Querying GitHub repositories, issues, and pull requests is permitted for reading.
rules.reason: Constitution explicitly permits querying GitHub repositories, issues, and pull requests for reading.

rules.name: escalate-github-write-operations
rules.description: Escalate all GitHub mutation operations for human approval.
rules.principle: No mutations: Any operation that creates, modifies, or deletes data must be escalated for human approval.
rules.reason: Constitution requires human approval before any write, create, delete, or push operation on GitHub.
